description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for time and calendar: reading time, AM and PM, elapsed time, calendar reading, and time and calendar word problems

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent clock and calendar foundation, can fast-track to elapsed time and word problems with challenges

videoSequence.reason: Quick introduction to reading time on an analog clock
AM and PM: understanding morning and afternoon/evening time
Elapsed time: calculating how much time has passed
Calendar reading: months, days, and the knuckle trick
Challenge: time and calendar word problems using number lines
Optional deeper explanation of telling time for enrichment

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of all time and calendar topics from basic reading to word problems

videoSequence.reason: Learn to read time on an analog clock step by step
Reinforce time-telling with a detailed explanation of clock mechanics
Understand AM and PM and how a day has two 12-hour halves
Learn to calculate elapsed time between two events
Learn to read a calendar: months, days, and the knuckle trick
Solve time and calendar word problems using number lines

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in clock reading or calendar vocabulary, needs targeted review before main content

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Reading the hour hand on a clock
Review: Skip counting by 5s for reading minutes
Start main topic: Learn to read time on an analog clock
AM and PM: morning vs afternoon/evening time
Elapsed time: how much time has passed
Calendar reading: months, days, and the knuckle trick

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in clock basics, skip counting, and calendar vocabulary before attempting time and calendar concepts

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Learn to read the hour hand on a clock
Essential: Skip counting by 5s for reading minutes
Critical: Days of the week and months of the year
Gentle intro to reading time on an analog clock
Fun calendar song to learn months and days

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these review videos, try the readiness quiz again to check if you are ready to continue with all time and calendar topics.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

hour_hand.conceptName: Reading the hour hand on a clock

days_months.conceptName: Days of the week and months of the year

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: If the short hand is on 7 and the long hand is on 12, what time is it?
questions.explanation: When the long hand is on 12, it means exactly on the hour. The short hand on 7 means it is 7:00 (7 o'clock).

questions.question: The long hand on a clock tells us the:
questions.explanation: The long (minute) hand tells us the minutes. The short hand tells us the hours.

ifAllCorrect.message: Great job! You understand how to read the clock really well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with identifying clock hands and reading time

questions.question: A movie starts at 3:00 PM and ends at 5:00 PM. How long is the movie?
questions.explanation: From 3:00 PM to 5:00 PM is 2 hours. Count: 3 to 4 is 1 hour, 4 to 5 is another hour. Total = 2 hours.

ifCorrect.message: Well done! You understand elapsed time.

ifWrong.message: Try counting the hours between start time and end time on your fingers. Go one hour at a time.

skip_ahead.action: Skip to word problems for extra challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on clock basics and skip counting

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising with a real clock at home: read the time every hour and write it down

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You already know your clock hands and calendar basics really well. You are ready to master telling time, elapsed time, and calendar skills. We have a fast-track playlist with some fun challenges!

good_score.message: Good work! You understand clock basics and calendar vocabulary nicely. Let's learn reading time, AM and PM, elapsed time, calendar reading, and how to solve time word problems.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in clock reading or calendar basics. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move on to the chapter topics.

low_score.message: You need to review clock hands, counting by 5s, and days and months first. We have created a playlist with fun videos to build a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn all about time and calendars.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how time and calendar concepts build on each other

concepts: Reading time in hours and minutes
Quarter past, half past, quarter to

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

clockReading.description: % of students who can correctly read time on an analog clock

calendarSkills.description: % of students who can correctly read dates and count days on a calendar

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (15 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 45-55 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing the hour hand with the minute hand
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasize: short hand = hours, long hand = minutes. Use coloring activities to mark each hand differently
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students reading minutes directly from the number instead of multiplying by 5
commonChallenges.solution2: Practice skip counting by 5s around the clock face until it becomes automatic
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students mixing up AM and PM
commonChallenges.solution3: Use daily routine anchors: breakfast = AM, dinner = PM. Draw a timeline of their day split into AM and PM halves
